nodes.x: TO the chief Musician upon Nehiloth, A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.
PSALM V. God favoureth not the wicked—David prayeth unto God: to guide him. * the chief Musician upon Nehiloth, : oo A’ Psalm of’ David. IVE -ear ‘to: my words, 0: Lord; consider my meditation.
Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
110 Verily I say unto you, even now, if he will hearken unto my voice, it shall be well with him. Even so. Amen.

6. And even so will I gather mine elect from the four quarters of
 the earth, even as many as will believe in me, and hearken unto my
 voice:
My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning will I direct [my prayer] unto thee, and will look up.
For thou [art] not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.
And God said unto Jacob (Gen. 35:1). Scripture states elsewhere in allusion to this verse: Thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness; evil shall not sojourn with Thee (Ps. 5:5).

nodes.lb: Kabbalah: Reshit Chokhmah, Gate of Holiness 17:6
nodes.x: In the Zohar they were very stringent about this sin, and this is what it says: (Zohar with Sulam commentary, Beresheet B) 393. Of the verse, "For You are not El who has pleasure in wickedness; nor shall evil dwell with you" (Tehilim 5:5), Rabbi Yehuda said: This verse has been discussed and explained, yet come and behold: he who cleaves to and is led by the Evil Inclination is defiled and will…

For indeed all the other sins of man, involving but his own corruption, admit of repentance. But the sin of onanism is one by which man corrupts both himself and the earth; and of such a one it is written, “The stain of thine iniquity remains before me” (Jer. 2, 22), also “For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness, evil shall not sojourn with thee” (Ps. 5, 5), and it is further…
The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.
Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.
But as for me, I will come [into] thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: [and] in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.
Lead me, O LORD, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.

BRING FORTH. Haytze (bring forth) is written as if it came from a root whose radicals remain unchanged in conjugation.39In reality it is a peh yod, and in hifil the yod should change to a vav (Filwarg). Hayshar (make straight) in Make Thy way straight before my face (Ps. 5:9) is similar.
For [there is] no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part [is] very wickedness; their throat
1. I will sing of the Lord's hesed forever; to all generations I will proclaim your faithfulness with my mouth.
Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.
But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
For thou, LORD, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as [with] a shield.
But Noah, who was righteous, found favour before the Lord.
